subject: Public Health
title: Incidence vs Prevalence
description: A short lesson to help you apply Incidence vs Prevalence.

hook.text: Incidence counts new cases. Prevalence counts all existing cases. Confusing them causes bad policy.

buildup.text: If 100 people catch flu this week, that's incidence. If 500 currently have it, that's prevalence.

discovery.text: A disease with high incidence but low prevalence resolves quickly—like a cold.

twist.text: A disease with low incidence but high prevalence is chronic—like diabetes, which accumulates over time.

climax.text: Knowing which metric to use determines whether you invest in prevention or long-term care.

punchline.text: New cases tell you speed. Total cases tell you burden.

quiz.question: What does incidence measure?
options: New cases over a specific time period
All existing cases at a point in time
The death rate from a disease
